steps.title: Define the goal
steps.content: The student wants to define the term georeferencing, so the right sentence must explain what the process is. The notes give that definition directly: georeferencing assigns geographic coordinates to an image so mapping software can place the image in its real-world location. Details about Puerto Rico, the 1930 photographs, and the 2017 project are useful context, but they do not by themselves define the term.

steps.title: Check each option
steps.content: Choice A focuses on a specific project and also makes assigning coordinates sound separate from georeferencing, even though that assignment is the process itself. Choice B says Lopez Marrero and colleagues used georeferencing, but it never explains what the word means. Choice C explains why georeferenced photographs can help researchers study coastline changes, which is a benefit rather than a definition. Choice D gives both parts needed for the definition: coordinates are assigned to an image, and mapping software can place it in its real-world location. The answer is D.
